publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2025-05-30 02:21:21 UTC.
profiledesc.langusage: Description is written in: Undetermined, Code for undetermined script script.

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: Photographs, slides, posters, books, shirts and skateboard decks created by Larry Clark. Photographs are from projects including Tulsa, Teenage Lust, KIDS, and Larry Clark 1992, among others.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Larry Clark Archive, #8055. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Lawrence Donald "Larry" Clark was born in Tulsa, Oklahoma in 1943 and has worked as a film director, photographer, writer, and film producer best known for the movie Kids and the photography book Tulsa. His work documents illegal drug use, underage sex and violence, and especially youth who are part of specific subcultures like surfing, punk rock or skateboarding.

separatedmaterial.head: Separated Materials
separatedmaterial.p: 10 books cataloged individually in the Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections.

controlaccess.subject: Teenagers -- Drama.
Teenagers -- Drug use.
Drug addicts -- Oklahoma -- Tulsa -- Pictorial works.
Skateboarding -- Pictorial works.
Teenage boys -- United States -- Social conditions.
Punk rock music.

did.unittitle: Series I. Photographs by Larry Clark

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Photographs are arranged by the project with which they were published. Photographs related to similar prints in published projects are included in that series. Some photographs appear in multiple publications, these were arranged with the earliest publication. Photographs where the project wasn't clear were arranged by date.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: See Teenage Lust for more prints related to Tulsa, as some from this series were published only in this later publication.

did.unittitle: Jack and Lynn Johnson, Oklahoma City

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Title applied by Clark. These photographs were removed from an Ilford paper box originally labeled "old rejects". Possibly prints rejected for exhibition.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Some photographs from the Tulsa series appear here, as they were included in this publication.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Title applied by Clark. These photographs were removed from an Ilford paper box originally labeled "old rejects". Possibly prints rejected for exhibition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Title applied by Clark. These photographs were removed from an Ilford paper box originally labeled "old rejects". Possibly prints rejected for exhibition.

did.unittitle: Pre-KIDS fashion show and street scenes

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Title applied by Clark. Includes photographs of the cast of KIDS trying on clothing, as well as the cast in street scenes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Possible that these skateboarders are part of Clark's KIDS project.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Related to photographs published in Larry Clark 1992. Title applied by Clark. These photographs were removed from an Ilford paper box originally labeled "old rejects". Possibly prints rejected for exhibition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Promotional shots of the actors from Clark's Wassup Rockers film, photographed in Los Angeles.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Photographs are in their original numbered envelopes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Slides of Clark's work including Tulsa, Teenage Lust, and Larry Clark 1992.

did.unittitle: Grand Prix Festival du film Policier
Another Day in Paradise, un film de Larry Clark
Larry Clark: Binder with Photographs (Larry Clark wearing some of the t-shirts)
Series IV. Photographs from the collection of Larry Clark

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Three photographs from Gibson's "The Somnombulist" photo book and one photograph from "Deja-Vu". One photograph includes inscription to Larry Clark.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Additional photographs by Ralph Gibson are held in the collection of the Herbert F. Johnson Museum of Art, Cornell University.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Additional photographs by August Sander are held in the collection of the Herbert F. Johnson Museum of Art, Cornell University.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Additional photographs by Walker Evans are held in the collection of the Herbert F. Johnson Museum of Art, Cornell University.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Additional photographs by Manuel Alvarez Bravo are held in the collection of the Herbert F. Johnson Museum of Art, Cornell University.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Skateboard decks designed by Larry Clark.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Access restricted to the permission of the curator until completion of processing.
